item.title: Zara presents Calma, an immersive installation offering a restful, sensorial experience at Palazzina Appiani. The space transforms into a peaceful esc...
item.description:
Zara presents Calma, an immersive installation offering a restful, sensorial experience at Palazzina Appiani. The space transforms into a peaceful escape from the city’s crowds, set within the serenity of Parco Sempione.

The project reinterprets the logic of ancient Roman thermae as a contemporary system of retreat, unfolding through a sequence of environments structured to settle perception. Created in partnership with Crosby Studios, it invites visitors to pause, reflect, and immerse themselves in a moment of calm.

Salone del Mobile 2026 | April 21 — 26
Palazzina Appiani, Viale Giorgio Byron 2, Milan

Book your visit at zara.com/it

#zara

item.author: zara

item.title: ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 16 / DROP I
item.description:
ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 16 / DROP I

In kidswear, this widening is expressed through proportion and a refined approach to utility. Silhouettes are set and specific, holding their shape while remaining easy to wear. In the first drop, colour-blocked looks - such as a bright green trouser with a crisp blue top and neutral jacket - create clear, graphic outlines. In the second, that clarity diffuses: a striped dress with a generous patch pocket balances softness with structure. Across both, soft cottons, considered finishes, and robust construction support comfort and independence.

Creative Director: Karl Templer #karltempler
Photographer: Craig McDean @craigmcdeanstudio
Video: Ned Rogers @nedrogers
Styling: Karl Templer #karltempler
Make up: Mark Carrasquillo @markcarrasquillo
Hair: Lukas Tralmer @lukastralmer
Set Designer: Gainsbury&Whiting #gainsburyandwhiting
Casting Director: Piergiorgio @piergiorgio @dmcasting
Production: Gainsbury&Whiting

#zara #zarasrpls
item.author: zara

item.title: ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 16 / DROP I
item.description:
ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 16 / DROP I

In menswear, the emphasis is on fabric, detail and surface. Shirts, trousers and lightweight outer layers are refined through washed finishes, texture and check. Silhouettes remain familiar but are precisely adjusted, with trousers shaping proportion through cut and detail, and fabrication carrying variation through wash, texture and pattern. Shirts and lightweight layers introduce further nuance without disrupting the overall balance. The wardrobe extends through refinement: pieces that feel known, but register differently in wear.

Creative Director: Karl Templer #karltempler
Photographer: Craig McDean @craigmcdeanstudio
Video: Ned Rogers @nedrogers
Styling: Karl Templer #karltempler
Make up: Mark Carrasquillo @markcarrasquillo
Hair: Lukas Tralmer @lukastralmer
Set Designer: Gainsbury&Whiting #gainsburyandwhiting
Casting Director: Piergiorgio @piergiorgio @dmcasting
Production: Gainsbury&Whiting

#zara #zarasrpls
item.author: zara

item.title: ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 16 / DROP I
item.description:
ZARA SRPLS – CLLCTN 16 / DROP I

SRPLS configures the wardrobe as a system: rooted in utility, defined by material, and refined through use. In womenswear, colour drives the evolution. Deployed with precision, it defines the silhouette through clear combinations - green with blue, orange with ecru - before softening into gradients and ombré treatments. These are integrated into familiar forms including workwear trousers, structured tops, and adaptable outer layers, expanding the wardrobe without disrupting its coherence. Fabric and finish remain central, with tactile cottons, treated surfaces, and construction details anchoring each piece in utility.

Creative Director: Karl Templer #karltempler
Photographer: Craig McDean @craigmcdeanstudio
Video: Ned Rogers @nedrogers 
Styling: Karl Templer #karltempler
Make up: Mark Carrasquillo @markcarrasquillo
Hair: Lukas Tralmer @lukastralmer
Set Designer: Gainsbury&Whiting #gainsburyandwhiting
Casting Director: Piergiorgio @piergiorgio @dmcasting
Production: Gainsbury&Whiting

#zara #zarasrpls
item.author: zara
